note: Populated locations of the Churn Edge (Tier-1, chaos-dominant), reached over the pass from Kestrel's Roost. Design principle (Erik 2026): the dominant axis is chaos_order negative, but the SECONDARY and TERTIARY axes are deliberately varied and non-obvious per node — the world leans chaos here, but WHICH other force rides along is not symmetric. Each node is its own coordinate. axisVector order: [emotional_logical, falsehood_truth, demonic_angelic, violence_peace, concrete_abstract, mechanical_spiritual, chaos_order, dark_light, death_life, space_time, body_mind, destruction_creation]. Locations use the full location schema so the engine can load them as real places. Authored by Aevi (PO).

locations.name: Tumbledown
locations.descriptionSeed: A market that has never been in the same place twice — stalls, tents, and whole streets reassembling overnight by rules no one admits to. What rides along with the chaos here isn't danger, it's INVENTION: the Churnfolk make things, trade things, remake things, and the disorder is generative rather than violent. You can find anything in Tumbledown once. You can never find it twice.
locations.encounterFlavor: Generative disorder — an inventor demonstrating a device that works once, a trade that can only be made today, a street that wasn't here yesterday and won't be tomorrow, a festival that started for no reason and everyone joined.
questSeeds: A Churnfolk maker built something extraordinary and can't rebuild it — the market ate the method; recovering it means chasing a pattern that resists being pinned
Someone is trying to IMPOSE order on Tumbledown — fixing the market in place — and half the town is grateful and half is dying inside; the Lattice's reach is testing here
A stall you needed is gone and its keeper with it; finding a person in a place that rearranges itself is its own art

locations.name: Spindrift Hollow
locations.descriptionSeed: Here the chaos rides on TIME instead of matter — a hollow where moments don't queue politely. You'll meet someone before you're introduced, hear the end of a conversation first, wait an hour that costs a day or a day that costs an hour. The Hollow-folk have made a slow strange peace with it and speak in a grammar that assumes the listener already knows. What comes along with chaos here is the least obvious neighbor on the axis-map: space_time. No one would have guessed the next valley over from a trade-market would fold time.
locations.encounterFlavor: Time out of joint — a warning that arrives after the danger, a debt paid before it's owed, an old woman who is also the child playing by the well, a moment you have to live twice to leave.
questSeeds: A Hollow-child keeps giving warnings about things that haven't happened; some come true and the town can't tell prophecy from memory — and neither can she
Someone came to the Hollow to un-live a single moment and has been trying for years; helping them means deciding whether that mercy is even possible here
A traveler is stuck in a repeating hour and doesn't know it; breaking the loop from outside is delicate, and the Hollow may not want them released

locations.name: The Gralloch
locations.descriptionSeed: The dangerous face of the Churn — where the disorder rides VIOLENCE and something older and colder underneath it. A broken badland of feuding bands and worse things drawn to the lawlessness. Not every chaos-neighbor is generative Tumbledown or strange Spindrift; this one bites. The randomness of the axis-map put teeth here, and the wise cross it fast or not at all.
locations.encounterFlavor: Raw lawless danger — a band demanding tribute, a settlement that survives by being meaner than the badland, a thing the feuds woke that neither side will name, a mercy that gets someone killed.
questSeeds: Two bands are feuding over a well and a third thing neither will name is picking off both; ending the feud means facing what the violence is feeding
A settlement of people who fled here to be free is being extorted; they'd rather die than go back under a city, and they might
Something in the Gralloch's heart isn't a person and isn't quite a beast — the demonic_angelic thread here is faint but real, and it's getting stronger where blood spills

locations.name: The Wend
locations.descriptionSeed: The gentlest node of the Unspooling — a wandering road-people whose whole culture is graceful motion, reached where the chaos thins back toward habitable. What rides with the chaos here is warmth: the Wend-folk feel their way through life, mourn openly, celebrate easily, and take in strays of every kind. The natural first foothold for a traveler out of the Valley into the Churn — chaos with a human face, and the road home still findable.
locations.encounterFlavor: Warm restless motion — a caravan-wedding, a grief openly carried, a stranger welcomed and read at once, a song that everyone somehow knows, the offer to travel with them a while.
questSeeds: The Wend take you in and, over the road, decide whether you're kin — a slow relationship that opens the whole Reach if it lands
A Wend elder is dying and wants to reach a place they left as a child before the end; the journey there crosses every danger of the Churn
Someone raised by the Wend wants to leave for a settled life and the road-family is grieving it as a small death; a familiar shape in a strange land

asymmetry_note: The four Churn nodes share chaos_order-negative as dominant, but their SECOND axes are deliberately unalike: Tumbledown rides destruction_creation (generative disorder), Spindrift rides space_time (the non-obvious one — a time-folding hollow next door to a market), the Gralloch rides violence_peace + a demonic_angelic thread (the one with teeth), the Wend rides emotional_logical + death_life (chaos with a warm human face). Same dominant force, four different worlds — this is the randomness Erik wanted: you know the chaos is coming, you do NOT know what rides with it until you arrive. The engine can generate more Churn nodes by holding chaos_order dominant and rolling the secondary/tertiary axes.
